  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Complete absence of curing blankets or curing compound during the critical initial hydration phase",
    "High risk of plastic shrinkage cracking, reduced compressive strength, and low surface durability"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Perform non-destructive testing (e.g., rebound hammer or windsor probe) and core testing to verify actual compressive strength",
    "Apply a surface hardener or penetrant sealer if surface durability is compromised but structural capacity is met",
    "Reject and replace the affected concrete slab if core strength fails to meet design specifications"
  ],
  "rationale": "The complete absence of curing measures (blankets or chemical compounds) violates ACI 308 and ACI 302 standards. Proper curing is essential for cement hydration, strength gain, and durability. Without it, the concrete is highly susceptible to premature drying, cracking, and surface dusting, leading to a failed service state.",
  "references": [
    "ACI 308R-16 (Guide to External Curing of Concrete)",
    "ACI 302.1R (Guide to Concrete Floor and Slab Construction)"
  ]
}
